things.name: Dunfermline Abbey & Palace
things.description: Ancient Benedictine abbey and medieval palace ruins — burial place of Robert the Bruce and 22 other Scottish royals, once Scotland's capital

things.name: Carnegie Birthplace Museum
things.description: The humble Dunfermline cottage where Andrew Carnegie, the world's greatest philanthropist, was born in 1835

things.name: Pittencrieff Park & Glen
things.description: Spectacular 76-acre park gifted to the people of Dunfermline by Andrew Carnegie — walled garden, glen walks and peacocks

things.name: Deep Sea World (North Queensferry)
things.description: One of the UK's largest aquariums with the world's longest underwater walkthrough tunnel — sharks, piranhas and seahorses

things.name: Forth Bridge (UNESCO World Heritage)
things.description: Iconic Victorian cantilever railway bridge over the Firth of Forth — a UNESCO World Heritage Site and engineering masterpiece since 1890

things.name: Abbot House Heritage Centre
things.description: Scotland's oldest secular building (late 14th century), now a heritage centre exploring 1,000 years of Dunfermline's royal history

things.name: Knockhill Racing Circuit
things.description: Scotland's national motorsport venue hosting motorsport events, track days and an adrenalin-packed driving experience centre

things.name: Culross Palace & Village (NTS)
things.description: Scotland's best-preserved example of a 17th-century Scottish burgh — a golden palace and cobbled streets untouched by time

things.name: Dunfermline Museum & Small Gallery
things.description: Free local museum adjacent to the Abbey covering Dunfermline's textile heritage and local history

things.name: Loch Leven Castle & Nature Reserve (Day Trip)
things.description: Island castle where Mary Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1567 surrounded by one of Scotland's finest wetland nature reserves
